weather_report: Late morning in Subiaco arrives soft and grey, the kind of light rain that freckles shop windows and settles the dust. The sky is a uniform pewter, sun mostly withheld, so the UV sits low and gentle; even at midday it will stay more suggestion than glare. Showers look set to linger into the early afternoon, keeping the day muted and a touch cool, with edges blurred and streets quietly reflective. It’s not unpleasant so much as subdued, a day that asks for unhurried steps and a bit of care at the crossings. A light waterproof jacket and comfortable closed shoes will be your allies, with a small umbrella tucked away for the fickle moments. Consider a lingering coffee while the rain chatters on the awnings, a quick browse along Rokeby Road between breaks, or a tidy errand run that ends at the library or a matinee. If the drizzle pauses, take a brief, thoughtful walk beneath the trees and breathe in the clean, rain-smoothed air.
